module_description: Monitors a Proxmox VE cluster or node through its REST API: cluster quorum, Ceph health, node status and maintenance, physical network interfaces, storage (inactive or high usage), and pending package updates (alerting on security updates).

hints.enabled: Enable or disable monitoring for this Proxmox connection.
hints.description: Internal notes about this cluster (optional). Does not affect monitoring.
hints.threads: Number of Proxmox connections to check in parallel.
hints.alert: Consecutive connection failures before alerting (smooths transient API blips).
hints.timeout: Default API request timeout in seconds.
hints.host: Proxmox node address(es). List several (comma/space separated) for failover — a cluster has multiple nodes, so the check survives one going down. It queries the whole cluster through whichever node answers, and also auto-discovers and retries the cluster's node IPs.
hints.vip: Stable cluster address (floating virtual IP or FQDN) to reach the API, independent of the member nodes. Tried first; if it does not answer, it fails over to the member host addresses and the discovered node IPs. Leave blank to use only the member hosts.
hints.verify_ssl: Verify the API TLS certificate. Off by default since Proxmox uses a self-signed certificate.
hints.auth_method: API token (recommended: no stored password, scoped) or username + password.
hints.token_id: API token id, e.g. monitor@pve!servicesentry. Create it under Datacenter → Permissions → API Tokens with a read-only role (PVEAuditor).
hints.token_secret: API token secret (UUID shown once when the token is created). Stored encrypted.
hints.username: User and realm, e.g. root@pam or monitor@pve.
hints.mode: Create: create the read-only user, grant the role and (re)create the token. Renew: only rotate the token secret (the user and role must already exist).
hints.prov_user: Read-only API user to create (user@realm). Default servicesentry@pve.
hints.prov_token: Name of the API token to create under that user.
hints.prov_role: Custom Proxmox role created with the minimum privileges the module needs (Sys.Audit) and assigned to the user. Created/updated if missing. Tighter than the built-in PVEAuditor role.
hints.ssh_cred_uid: Optionally reuse a saved SSH credential for the SSH login; otherwise fill the SSH user/password below.
hints.ssh_user: SSH user on the Proxmox node used to provision the token (root, or a user that can run pveum, e.g. via sudo). If left blank and the check is bound to a host, the host's SSH profile user is used; otherwise root. Overridden by the selected SSH credential.
hints.ssh_port: SSH port of the Proxmox node. If left blank and the check is bound to a host, the host's SSH profile port is used; otherwise 22.
hints.ssh_password: SSH password (used when no SSH credential is selected). Stored encrypted.
hints.ssh_key: Path (on the ServiceSentry server) to an SSH private key, as an alternative to the password.
hints.check_cluster: Check cluster quorum (corosync). A standalone node reports as online without a cluster.
hints.check_nodes: Check each node: online/offline and whether it is in HA maintenance mode.
hints.check_ceph: Check Ceph health (HEALTH_OK / WARN / ERR). Reports 'not configured' when Ceph is not installed.
hints.check_network: Check each node's physical network interfaces: alert when an autostart interface is down.
hints.check_updates: Check pending apt updates per node. Alerts on security updates; informs when the count reaches the threshold. Requires the Sys.Modify privilege (Proxmox gates the apt/update list behind it) — enable it during token provisioning or grant it manually.
hints.updates_threshold: Number of pending updates from which the check reports them (informational). Security updates always alert regardless of this value.
hints.check_storage: Check each node's storage: alert when an enabled storage is inactive (down) or its usage exceeds the threshold. Requires the Datastore.Audit privilege.
hints.storage_threshold: Usage percentage above which a storage is alerted (0 = do not alert on usage, only on inactive).
hints.check_permissions: Verify the token holds every privilege the enabled checks need (Sys.Audit; Datastore.Audit for storage; Sys.Modify for updates). Missing ones are reported as a warning naming the privilege.
hints.allow_updates: Also grant Sys.Modify to the provisioned role so the updates check can read the apt list. Broadens the token (node-modify): enable only if you'll use the updates check.
